steps.title: Recognize vertex form and read vertex coordinates directly
steps.content: The equation $y = -16(x - 5.6)^2 + 502$ is already in vertex form $y = a(x - h)^2 + k$. From the structure of the equation, the vertex is at $(h, k) = (5.6, 502)$.

steps.title: Interpret the vertex in context and select the answer
steps.content: In this problem, $x$ represents seconds after launch and $y$ represents height in feet. Since the coefficient $a = -16 < 0$, the parabola opens downward, so the vertex represents the maximum height. The vertex $(5.6, 502)$ means the rocket reaches a maximum height of $502$ feet at $5.6$ seconds after launch. That matches choice B — B is correct.
